title: Design the Book Entity Class
description: Create a `Book` class with fields: `isbn` (String), `title` (String), `author` (String), `genre` (String), `totalCopies` (int), `availableCopies` (int), `yearPublished` (int). Add a constructor, getters, setters, and override `toString()`, `equals()`, and `hashCode()` based on ISBN.
acceptanceCriteria: Book class compiles without errors
All fields have correct types and access modifiers
Constructor validates that totalCopies >= 1
equals() and hashCode() are based on ISBN
toString() produces a readable output
hints: Use `Objects.requireNonNull()` for null validation in the constructor
ISBN should be unique — base equals/hashCode on it
Use `@Override` annotation when overriding methods

title: Design the Member Entity Class
description: Create a `Member` class with fields: `memberId` (String), `name` (String), `email` (String), `phone` (String), `membershipDate` (LocalDate), `activeBorrows` (List<String> — ISBNs). Add validation in the constructor and override `equals/hashCode` on memberId.
acceptanceCriteria: Member class compiles without errors
email field is validated (not null or empty)
activeBorrows initializes as an empty ArrayList
equals/hashCode based on memberId
toString() shows name and memberId
hints: Use `java.time.LocalDate` for the membership date (not java.util.Date)
Initialize activeBorrows with `new ArrayList<>()` in constructor
Validate email is not blank with `if (email == null || email.isBlank())`

title: Design the BorrowRecord Entity
description: Create a `BorrowRecord` class with: `recordId` (UUID), `memberId` (String), `isbn` (String), `borrowDate` (LocalDate), `dueDate` (LocalDate, auto-set to borrowDate + 14 days), `returnDate` (LocalDate, nullable), `isReturned` (boolean).
acceptanceCriteria: BorrowRecord class compiles without errors
recordId is auto-generated using UUID.randomUUID()
dueDate defaults to borrowDate + 14 days
returnDate is null for active borrows
isOverdue() method returns true if not returned and today > dueDate
hints: Use `UUID.randomUUID().toString()` for auto-generated IDs
Use `borrowDate.plusDays(14)` to compute dueDate
isOverdue() should check: `!isReturned && LocalDate.now().isAfter(dueDate)`

title: Implement BookCatalogueService (CRUD)
description: Create a `BookCatalogueService` class backed by a `HashMap<String, Book>` (keyed by ISBN). Implement: `addBook(Book)`, `removeBook(String isbn)`, `getBook(String isbn)`, `listAllBooks()`, and handle duplicate ISBNs with a custom `DuplicateBookException`.
acceptanceCriteria: HashMap used for O(1) ISBN lookup
addBook throws DuplicateBookException if ISBN already exists
removeBook throws BookNotFoundException if ISBN not found
listAllBooks returns an unmodifiable List<Book>
Custom exceptions extend RuntimeException with meaningful messages
hints: Use `HashMap<String, Book>` with ISBN as key
Use `containsKey()` to check for duplicate before inserting
Return `Collections.unmodifiableList(new ArrayList<>(catalogue.values()))` from listAllBooks()

title: Implement Multi-Criteria Search
description: Add a `search(SearchCriteria criteria)` method to `BookCatalogueService` using Java Streams. Support filtering by title (contains, case-insensitive), author, genre, and year range.
acceptanceCriteria: Search uses Java Streams with filter() and collect()
Title search is case-insensitive using toLowerCase()
Multiple criteria can be combined (AND logic)
Empty criteria returns all books
Returns an unmodifiable list of results
hints: Use `catalogue.values().stream().filter(...).collect(Collectors.toList())`
Use `Optional<String>` in SearchCriteria so fields can be absent
Chain multiple `.filter()` calls for AND logic

title: Implement MemberService (CRUD)
description: Create a `MemberService` class backed by `HashMap<String, Member>`. Implement `registerMember(Member)`, `removeMember(String memberId)`, `getMember(String memberId)`, and `listAllMembers()`.
acceptanceCriteria: HashMap used for member storage keyed by memberId
registerMember throws DuplicateMemberException if memberId exists
getMember throws MemberNotFoundException if not found
listAllMembers returns an unmodifiable list
hints: Follow the same pattern as BookCatalogueService
Member IDs should be human-readable (e.g., M001, M002)

title: Implement Borrow Operation
description: Create a `LibraryService` class with a `borrowBook(String memberId, String isbn)` method. Validate: book exists, book has available copies, member has not exceeded their borrow limit (max 3 books). Create a `BorrowRecord`, decrement `availableCopies`, and update the member's `activeBorrows`.
acceptanceCriteria: Throws BookNotAvailableException when no copies remain
Throws BorrowLimitExceededException when member has 3 active borrows
BorrowRecord is created and stored
availableCopies decremented on the Book
isbn added to member's activeBorrows list
hints: Check `book.getAvailableCopies() <= 0` for unavailability
Check `member.getActiveBorrows().size() >= 3` for limit
Wrap all operations in try-catch and throw custom exceptions

title: Implement Return & Fine Calculation
description: Add a `returnBook(String memberId, String isbn)` method to `LibraryService`. Mark the `BorrowRecord` as returned. If the book is overdue, compute a fine at ₹5/day. Increment `availableCopies`. Remove isbn from `activeBorrows`.
acceptanceCriteria: BorrowRecord.returnDate set to today
BorrowRecord.isReturned set to true
availableCopies incremented
isbn removed from member's activeBorrows
Fine computed correctly: overdueDays * 5
Return value includes fine amount (0 if returned on time)
hints: Use `ChronoUnit.DAYS.between(record.getDueDate(), LocalDate.now())` for overdue days
Only compute fine if `returnDate.isAfter(dueDate)`
Return a `ReturnReceipt` object containing fine amount and record

title: Implement Overdue Report
description: Add a `getOverdueReport()` method to `LibraryService`. Return a list of all active borrow records where `isOverdue()` is true, enriched with member name, book title, days overdue, and fine amount.
acceptanceCriteria: Returns only active (not returned) overdue records
Each report entry includes: memberName, bookTitle, daysOverdue, fine
Report is sorted by daysOverdue descending
Empty list returned if no overdue records
hints: Filter: `records.stream().filter(r -> !r.isReturned() && r.isOverdue())`
Sort with `.sorted(Comparator.comparingLong(this::computeOverdueDays).reversed())`

title: Implement File Persistence
description: Create a `DataPersistenceService` that serializes the catalogue, members, and borrow records to disk as JSON files using the Jackson library (or manual serialization). Load data on startup and save on shutdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Books, members, and borrow records persisted to separate JSON files
Data loaded correctly on application startup
Data saved correctly on application shutdown (or after each write)
Application handles missing data files gracefully (starts with empty state)
Invalid JSON files produce a clear error message
hints: Use Jackson's `ObjectMapper` for JSON serialization/deserialization
Add Jackson dependency: `com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind`
Use a `@JsonDeserialize` annotation for LocalDate if using Jackson

title: Build the Interactive CLI Menu
description: Create a `CLIApplication` main class with a loop that reads user input and dispatches to the appropriate service methods. Menu: 1) Add Book, 2) Search Books, 3) Register Member, 4) Borrow Book, 5) Return Book, 6) Overdue Report, 7) Exit.
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 menu options implemented and working
Scanner reads user input correctly
Invalid input shows an error and re-displays the menu
Exit option saves data before quitting
Menu is clearly formatted and readable
hints: Use `Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in)` for input
Use a `while(true)` loop broken by the Exit option
Handle `InputMismatchException` when reading integers

title: Write JUnit Tests for Business Logic
description: Write unit tests for: BookCatalogueService (add, remove, search), LibraryService (borrow, return, overdue), and fine calculation. Use JUnit 5 with @Test, @BeforeEach, and assertions.
acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 10 test methods covering happy paths and exception scenarios
Tests use @BeforeEach to reset service state
Assertions use assertThrows() for exception testing
All tests pass (green) when running `mvn test` or `./gradlew test`
hints: Use `assertThrows(BookNotFoundException.class, () -> service.removeBook("unknown"))` pattern
Set up a fresh service instance in @BeforeEach
Test boundary conditions (borrow limit of 3, zero available copies)

title: SOLID Principles Self-Review
description: Review each class against the 5 SOLID principles. Document at least one example of each principle in your codebase in a `DESIGN_NOTES.md` file.
acceptanceCriteria: DESIGN_NOTES.md created in project root
One example of Single Responsibility Principle identified
One example of Open/Closed Principle identified
One example of Liskov Substitution Principle identified
One example of Interface Segregation Principle identified
One example of Dependency Inversion Principle identified
hints: SRP: Each service class handles one domain (BookCatalogueService != LibraryService)
DIP: Services depend on abstractions (interfaces) not concrete implementations
OCP: Adding a new search criterion doesn't require modifying existing search logic
